signals.title: USD/JPY — Fed Hawkish Tilt + BoJ Intervention Risk + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 160.57, trading above the 160.00 level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings. The Fed's hawkish tilt, signaling potential hikes later this year despite holding rates, drives USD strength. This is reinforced by the 290bp SOFR-TONA carry premium favoring USD. JPY COT LF net short −115,036 (29.2% long) indicates crowded speculative short positioning, increasing short-squeeze risk if BoJ intervenes or shifts policy. Options market aligns with a moderate put skew (USDJPY 25d RR at -1.09), indicating demand for JPY upside protection. Watch for sustained BoJ verbal intervention or a shift in the BoJ's next meeting on 31 Jul.

signals.title: USD/CAD — G10 Bilateral Theme + Carry + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD is the strongest G10 currency this week (+0.503% vs peers) while CAD is the weakest (−0.653%), creating a 1.156% leader-to-laggard spread. This bilateral theme is driven by the 138bp SOFR-CORRA carry premium favoring USD. USD/CAD COT LF net short −60,671 (24.7% long) indicates crowded speculative short positioning, while retail sentiment is extremely short at 95% (Myfxbook). This institutional vs. retail divergence suggests potential for a short-squeeze if price extends higher. USD/CAD at 1.4099. Watch for CAD data surprises or BoC policy shifts at the 15 Jul meeting.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility Premium + Negative CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD IV at 26.1% vs HV30 at 7.8% results in an extreme +18.3% VRP, indicating strong hedging demand for AUD protection significantly above realized volatility. This elevated options premium suggests market participants are pricing in substantial downside risk. The AUD CESI net score of −8 (moderate negative bias) confirms data is consistently disappointing expectations, adding fundamental pressure. AUD/USD at 0.7041. Watch for RBA's next meeting on 11 Aug for any shift in policy bias.

signals.title: NZD/USD — Retail Extreme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/USD retail sentiment shows an extreme 90% long bias (Myfxbook), indicating a crowded retail position. This contrasts with the RBNZ's market bias for a hike, which is likely to trigger a long-unwind if the RBNZ delivers or signals a hawkish shift. NZD COT LF net short −22,354 (23.9% long) also suggests institutional shorts are fading the retail long. NZD/USD at 0.5797. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 20% hike priced) on 8 Jul is the key catalyst for this divergence.
